title: Override Default Falco Rule Priority
description:
## Override Default Falco Rule Priority

Organization-specific compliance regulations may require tuning security priorities. In this task, you will override the priority of a built-in Falco rule to ensure it triggers at a higher severity level.

**Your task:**

Create a Kubernetes ConfigMap named `falco-rule-overrides` in the namespace `falco` containing overrides for built-in rules.
- The ConfigMap must have a key named `overrides.yaml` containing a YAML list.
- It must override the existing rule named `Run shell untrusted` to set its `priority` to `CRITICAL`.

```yaml
# Expected structure inside overrides.yaml:
- rule: Run shell untrusted
  priority: CRITICAL
```

hints.title: Creating the override ConfigMap
hints.body: Define the ConfigMap with overrides.yaml containing the overridden rule block:
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: falco-rule-overrides
  namespace: falco
data:
  overrides.yaml: |
    - rule: Run shell untrusted
      priority: CRITICAL
EOF

commands.description: ConfigMap falco-rule-overrides exists in namespace falco
commands.command: kubectl get configmap falco-rule-overrides -n falco -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'

commands.description: ConfigMap contains overrides.yaml with Run shell untrusted priority set to CRITICAL
commands.command: python3 -c 'import yaml, subprocess; out = subprocess.check_output(["kubectl", "get", "configmap", "falco-rule-overrides", "-n", "falco", "-o", "jsonpath={.data.overrides\\.yaml}"]); data = yaml.safe_load(out); rules = [x for x in data if isinstance(x, dict) and x.get("rule") == "Run shell untrusted"]; print("ok" if rules and rules[0].get("priority") == "CRITICAL" else "fail")'
